settings.title: Settings
settings.copy_warning: Copy the key now — it won't be shown again:
settings.license_desc: Manage your subscription and usage limits.
settings.group_limit_reached: Group limit reached (1 max on Free plan). Enter a license key to add more.
settings.account_limit_reached: Account limit reached (5 max on Free plan). Enter a license key to add more.
settings.resolution_failed: Could not determine server settings automatically. Please enter them manually.
settings.mark_read_delay_desc: Time after which a message is marked as read when opened
settings.about_update_channel: Update channel baked into this installation
settings.name: Name
settings.reset_sync_confirm: Sync state will be reset and ALL messages will be re-downloaded. Old messages will be re-processed (attachments, AI, etc.). Continue?
settings.reset_sync_done: Sync reset — messages will reload on next cycle
settings.pause_sync_done: Sync paused — account will not connect to mail server
settings.resume_sync_done: Sync resumed — account will reconnect shortly
settings.contacts_confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this contact?
settings.telegram_enabled_desc: Enable automatic notification delivery for new incoming emails
settings.telegram_ai_notifications_desc: AI analyzes the subject and content of each email, sending a structured digest with importance ratings (🔴 Critical, 🟡 Important, 🟢 Normal).
settings.telegram_ai_chat_desc: Manage your AI assistant directly from Telegram chat: request summaries of recent emails, ask questions about your inbox.
settings.telegram_setup_title: How to set up Telegram integration
settings.telegram_step1: Find the @userinfobot (or @KeepSilenceBot) bot in Telegram to get your unique Telegram User ID.
settings.telegram_step2: Enter your ID in the field below, configure the toggles and press "Save".
settings.telegram_step3: Send the /start command to your Telegram bot to activate features and verify the connection.
settings.notifications_blocked: Notifications are blocked. Please allow them in your browser settings (Safari → Settings → Websites → Notifications).
settings.import_mailboxes_desc: Local mailboxes were detected on this server. Select the ones you want to import:
settings.import_password_hint: Use the same password as for login.
settings.password_too_short: Password must be at least 6 characters
settings.show_account_name: Show account name instead of email
settings.telegram_bot_configured_desc: To receive notifications and chat with the AI assistant, message the bot:
settings.telegram_bot_not_configured_desc: To activate the integration, create a bot in Telegram and enter its token below.
settings.telegram_bot_step1_desc: Find <link>@BotFather</link> in Telegram and start it.
settings.telegram_bot_step2_desc: Send the /newbot command, choose a name and a unique username for your bot.
settings.telegram_bot_step3_desc: Copy the HTTP API Token you receive and paste it into the "Bot Token" field below.
settings.telegram_header_desc: Manage your smart email assistant via Telegram messenger
settings.telegram_guide_step2_hint: Make sure the ID contains only digits.
settings.telegram_integration_params_desc: Set the recipient ID and choose which AI capabilities will be active.
settings.telegram_user_id_desc: Unique numeric identifier obtained from the bot. All notifications will be directed here.
settings.telegram_validation_invalid_id: Telegram User ID must be a valid positive number
settings.telegram_security_note_desc: The integration is completely safe. Access to email management and the AI assistant is granted only for the specified ID. Messages from other Telegram accounts will be ignored.
settings.confirm_reset_ai_stats: Are you sure you want to reset all AI statistics?
settings.fetch_models_error: Failed to fetch models for {provider}: {error}. Using fallback.
settings.fetch_models_error_fallback: Failed to fetch models for {provider}. Using fallback.
settings.webhooks_description: Manage webhooks to send email data to external services.
settings.managed_by_admin_desc: Settings are locked by corporate policy
settings.support_modal_desc: RMS Mail is open-source and free. If it saves you time or money, consider treating the author to a coffee!
settings.notification_no_release_notes: No release description provided for this version.
settings.oauth_byoa_desc: Configure your own Google and Microsoft OAuth applications to isolate API quotas and enhance security. Client secrets are securely masked after saving.
settings.oauth_redirect_desc: Copy this URL and paste it as the Authorized Redirect URI in your OAuth provider console.
settings.oauth_client_secret_desc: Leave blank or as ******** to keep the current secret unchanged.
settings.oauth_google_inst_prep_desc: In your OAuth tab, copy the Redirect URI. Usually it looks like: https://mail.your-domain.com/api/oauth/callback
settings.oauth_google_inst_2_desc: In the top left corner, click the projects dropdown and select New Project. Enter a name (e.g. RMS Mail) and click Create.
settings.oauth_google_inst_3_desc: Go to APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen. Select External and click Create. Fill in App name, User support email, and Developer contact information. Click Save and Continue. Under Scopes, add mail scopes (e.g. https://mail.google.com/, email, profile). Important: Return to OAuth consent screen and click Publish App, otherwise authorization expires in 7 days.
settings.oauth_google_inst_4_desc: Go to Credentials. Click + Create Credentials and select OAuth client ID. Select Web application as type. Under Authorized redirect URIs, click + Add URI and paste your Redirect URI. Click Create.
settings.oauth_google_inst_5_desc: A window will appear with your Client ID and Client Secret. Copy and paste them into the settings here.
settings.oauth_ms_inst_1_desc: Go to Azure Portal and log in with your Microsoft account.
settings.oauth_ms_inst_2_desc: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory).
settings.oauth_ms_inst_3_desc: Go to App registrations and click + New registration. Enter a name. Under Supported account types, select the third option (Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts). Under Redirect URI, select Web and paste your Redirect URI. Click Register.
settings.oauth_ms_inst_4_desc: After creation, copy the Application (client) ID value — this is your Client ID.
settings.oauth_ms_inst_5_desc: Go to Certificates & secrets. Under Client secrets, click + New client secret. Add a description and expiration. Important: Copy the Value immediately (not the Secret ID!). It is shown only once.
settings.oauth_ms_inst_6_desc: Go to API permissions. Click + Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Delegated permissions. Add mail permissions (e.g. IMAP.AccessAsUser.All, SMTP.Send, offline_access, User.Read). Click Add permissions.
settings.premium_subtitle: Get unlimited accounts, groups, and priority support
settings.delete_confirm_desc: This action will permanently delete the folder "{folder}" and all emails inside it, both locally and on the remote IMAP server. This action cannot be undone.
settings.admin_access_desc: You do not have permission to view or edit admin settings.
settings.admin_allowed_domains_desc: Limit account registration to specific domains (e.g. example.com). Leave empty to allow any domain.
